sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: VfL Wolfsburg, sitting at 12th in the league with 39 points, host SC Freiburg, who are in an impressive 4th position with 52 points, in Round 31 at the Volkswagen Arena in Wolfsburg. This matchup promises to be an intriguing clash between a mid-table side looking to climb and a top-tier team aiming to solidify their standing. The referee for this encounter, Sven Jablonski, averages 3.9 yellow cards and 0.15 red cards per game, suggesting a moderate likelihood of disciplinary actions.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head History
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these teams, SC Freiburg holds a slight edge with 3 wins compared to Wolfsburg's 2, with no draws recorded. Their most recent encounter on December 13, 2024, saw Freiburg triumph 3-2 at home, with Lukas Kübler earning Player of the Match honors with a stellar 8.7 Sofascore rating. Historically, Freiburg has been first to score in all 5 of these recent games, a streak that continues to this day, while matches between these sides often see over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: VfL Wolfsburg are in a troubling run of form, currently on a streak of 6 games without a win and 10 games without a clean sheet, both ongoing. They’ve also been the first to concede in 5 of their last 6 matches, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. On the other hand, SC Freiburg have had a mixed defensive record with no clean sheet in their last 6 away games, though this streak has recently been broken. Freiburg’s games often feature over 2.5 goals and both teams scoring in 6 of their last 6 away matches, though these trends have not continued in their most recent outings.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - VfL Wolfsburg
sections.text: For Wolfsburg, forward Mohamed El Amine Amoura stands out with 10 goals and 8 assists this season, overperforming his expected goals (xG) of 8.66 by 1.34. Jonas Wind, another key forward, has contributed 8 goals despite an xG of 5.34, showcasing his clinical finishing with a 2.66 overperformance. Defender Denis Vavro also merits attention, ranking 4th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes at 10%, which could play a role in Wolfsburg’s build-up play. Midfielder Maximilian Arnold, with 3 goals and 5 assists, brings experience to the midfield with a solid 7.13 average rating.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - SC Freiburg
sections.text: SC Freiburg boasts some top talent, with midfielder Vincenzo Grifo ranking 2nd in the league for assists with 10 and 4th for big chances created with 15, alongside an impressive 7.31 average rating. Grifo also ranks 5th for key passes with 61, making him a creative force. Ritsu Doan, another midfielder, has been in form over the last 5 matches with a 7.02 average rating, scoring 1 goal and providing 3 assists. Defender Max Rosenfelder, ranking 2nd in the league for ground duels won at 72.2%, adds defensive steel to Freiburg’s backline.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - VfL Wolfsburg
sections.text: VfL Wolfsburg have scored 53 goals this season, ranking 7th in the league, but their defense has struggled, conceding 52 goals and ranking 12th for least goals conceded. They’ve underperformed their expected goals by -7.23, ranking 15th for goals vs. xG difference. Their strength lies in late-game scoring, with 17.3% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Wolfsburg also rank well in defensive stats, with 3rd in interceptions per game at 9.16 and 4th in clearances per game at 27.47, which could be crucial against Freiburg’s attack.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights - SC Freiburg
sections.text: SC Freiburg have shown attacking intent, with significant portions of their goals coming in the latter stages of games, including 24.5% between the 61st and 75th minutes and another 24.5% between the 76th and 90th minutes. Their defense has been leaky on the road recently, though they’ve managed to score consistently. Their ability to create big chances could test Wolfsburg’s shaky backline, especially considering Wolfsburg’s ongoing 10-game streak without a clean sheet. Freiburg’s matches also tend to have fewer than 10.5 corners, a trend holding in 9 of their last 9 away games.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Disciplinary actions could play a role, as head-to-head stats show over 4.5 cards in 4 of the last 5 meetings between these teams, a trend that continues. Wolfsburg average 2.34 yellow cards per game, ranking 17th for least yellow cards, while referee Sven Jablonski’s 3.9 yellow cards per game suggest bookings are likely. Set-piece battles may be less frequent, as both teams have seen under 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads, and Wolfsburg average 4.69 corners per game, ranking 9th in the league.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: VfL Wolfsburg will miss key midfielder Patrick Wimmer, who has 3 goals and 4 assists, alongside Mattias Svanberg and defender Sebastiaan Bornauw, potentially weakening their midfield and defense. SC Freiburg, meanwhile, will be without midfielder Eren Dinkçi, who has contributed 3 assists this season, which could slightly impact their attacking fluidity. These absences might tilt the balance, especially for Wolfsburg, who are already struggling defensively and in form.
